channel.title: Brazil Elections 2026
channel.description: Real-time monitoring of the 2026 Brazilian presidential elections.

item.title: PT 'torce' por candidatura de Kim ao Governo de SP para viabilizar segundo turno
item.description: PT leaders are discreetly rooting for Kim Kataguiri's gubernatorial candidacy in São Paulo, hoping his presence in a runoff would split the right-wing vote and help Fernando Haddad defeat Tarcísio de Freitas. The strategy aims to prevent a first-round victory by the current governor. The report was published on March 15, 2026.

item.title: Flávio rebate críticas por animação em evento: ‘Fazendo o que meu pai pediu’
item.description: Senator Flávio Bolsonaro defended himself against criticism for appearing cheerful and energetic at a political event in Rondônia, stating he was following his father Jair Bolsonaro’s request to bring hope to the Brazilian people. He said he went directly to the hospital afterward to inform his father about the successful launch of the pre-candidacies of Marcos Rogério, Fernando Máximo, and Bruno Sheidt, noting that Jair Bolsonaro was pleased with the news. Despite the ex-president’s ongoing hospitalization for bilateral bacterial pneumonia in the ICU, Flávio framed his attitude as an act of obedience and optimism.

item.title: Flávio tenta se explicar após dança em palanque?
item.description: Senator Flávio Bolsonaro (PL-RJ) posted a video on Sunday morning, the 15th, in which he attempts to explain himself after being criticized for dancing atop a platform during the event launching Senator Marcos Rogério’s (PL-RO) pre-candidacy for governor of Rondônia. The recording went viral on social media, sparking controversy as many viewed the act as inappropriate in a serious political context, especially given the implied presence of former President Jair Bolsonaro. Flávio seeks to mitigate reputational damage, claiming the moment was lighthearted and not intended to disrespect the event.

item.title: Sem observar Neymar, Ancelotti convoca para últimos testes antes da lista final
item.description: Carlo Ancelotti will reveal this Monday (16) the final call-up for the friendlies against France and Croatia, which will serve as last tests before the World Cup 2026 roster is set. Neymar, despite 12 days of preparation, did not participate in tactical training against Mirassol due to muscle fatigue and was rested, sparking criticism on social media. The Italian coach faces ongoing pressure regarding Neymar’s inclusion, as the player has yet to play under his leadership.

item.title: Seleção brasileira feminina derrota Mali e permanece com chance no Pré-Mundial de basquete
item.description: The Brazilian women's basketball team defeated Mali 76-73 in Wuhan, rallying in the final quarter to keep alive their hopes of qualifying for the 2026 FIBA World Cup in Germany. Brazil must now beat China on Tuesday or rely on other results to secure one of the three available spots. Kamilla Cardoso led Brazil with 17 points and 11 rebounds, while Bella Nascimento scored all 12 of her points in the final period to spark the comeback.

item.title: França registra participação abaixo de 20% nas horas iniciais do 1° turno das eleições municipais
item.description: Voter turnout in France's municipal elections first round was below 20% in the initial hours, nearing the 2020 record low of 18.38%. Nearly 49 million voters were called to the polls on Sunday, but the weak participation reflects a broader trend of electoral disengagement in recent French local elections.

item.title: Um cenário insólito para a indicação de Jorge Messias ao Supremo
item.description: President Lula nominated Attorney General Jorge Messias for the Supreme Federal Court in November, but the appointment remains stalled due to a political impasse with Senator Davi Alcolumbre, who controls the Senate’s agenda. Alcolumbre may delay the vote until after the October elections to negotiate with the next president, echoing his past obstruction of Bolsonaro’s nomination of André Mendonça. Messias has sought senatorial support without success, while Lula is leveraging the promise of a future STF nomination for Rodrigo Pacheco to encourage him to run for governor of Minas Gerais.

item.title: Defesa aguarda novo laudo para pedir prisão domiciliar de Bolsonaro, diz Flávio
item.description: Senator Flávio Bolsonaro (PL-RJ) stated that the defense of former President Jair Bolsonaro is awaiting a new medical report to request house arrest again. The comment was made following a visit to his father, who is hospitalized at DF Star Hospital in Brasília.

item.title: STF avança em investigações contra Lulinha e políticos que desviaram emendas
item.description: The STF is accelerating investigations against politicians, including Lula's son (Lulinha) and the INSS Careca, with evidence of influence peddling. Linked to the Banco Master scandal, the probe aims to imprison those responsible this semester, avoiding interference in the 2026 electoral campaign.

item.title: ‘Alcolumbre precisa pedir para sair’, diz senador Eduardo Girão
item.description: Senator Eduardo Girão (Novo-CE) urged Senate President Davi Alcolumbre (União Brasil-AP) to resign for obstructing the installation of a parliamentary inquiry into the Banco Master scandal, despite having 51 signatures. He also renewed calls for the impeachment of Supreme Court Minister Alexandre de Moraes, citing contracts between Moraes' wife's law firm and the bank, and alleged messages with the bank's owner, arguing the scandal has broken through right-wing echo chambers and intensified pressure on the Court.

item.title: Temer participa de debate sobre panorama eleitoral na Fiesp
item.description: Former Brazilian president Michel Temer (MDB) will participate in a debate at Fiesp on the Brazilian electoral outlook on March 16, 2026, alongside writer Denis Rosenfield and political scientist Rubens Figueiredo. The event will discuss election prospects, with Temer's allies encouraging him to run again for president as a way to reduce polarization between Lula and Flávio Bolsonaro.

item.title: Só 12% das 6.200 obras de educação do governo Lula já foram entregues
item.description: The Lula government has delivered only 12% of the 6,227 education projects planned for its term, with just one daycare center in Assararé (CE) completed out of 2,443 school construction proposals. Of the R$15 billion allocated under the Novo PAC for education, only R$1.2 billion (8%) has been paid, highlighting weak federal disbursement capacity and delays in bidding and execution. Despite 826,371 children waiting for daycare spots in 2025, progress remains slow, with projects concentrated in Bahia, Pernambuco, Ceará, Minas Gerais, and Maranhão.

item.title: 1ª Turma do STF julga recursos do “núcleo da desinformação”
item.description: The First Chamber of the STF ruled on embargos de declaração filed by those convicted in the 'disinformation nucleus,' which spread false news about electronic voting machines and attacked institutions during the 2022 coup attempt. The ministers upheld the sentences, ranging from 7 years and 6 months to 17 years in prison, with Alexandre de Moraes voting to reject all appeals and Cármen Lúcia joining him in the cases of Denicoli and Rocha.

item.title: Moro desafia Ciro em Sobral e relembra ameaça de ser recebido “na bala”
item.description: Sergio Moro challenged Ciro Gomes during an event in Sobral, recalling a 2017 statement in which Ciro said he would receive 'the gang on a bullet' referring to the then-federal judge. The challenge took place during the launch of Eduardo Girão's pre-candidacy for governor of Ceará. Moro used the moment to highlight past political tensions with the former minister.

item.title: Maioria quer Brasil neutro na guerra no Oriente Médio, diz pesquisa
item.description: A Genial/Quaest poll from March 2026 reveals that 77% of Brazilians support Lula’s government maintaining neutrality in the U.S.-Israel-Iran conflict, with only 14% backing the U.S. and Israel and 2% supporting Iran. Additionally, 81% fear the conflict could spread globally. The survey included 2,004 respondents from March 6–9, with a 2-point margin of error and 95% confidence level.

item.title: Datafolha: apoio ao fim da escala 6×1 sobe a 71%, após 64% na pesquisa anterior
item.description: Datafolha poll shows 71% of Brazilians support reducing the workweek from a 6×1 schedule (six days on, one off) to a 5×2 model (five days on, two off) without salary reduction, up from 64% in December 2024. Support is stronger among women (77%) and those working five or fewer days weekly (76%), and the proposal is backed by the Lula government as a way to ease burdens, particularly for women. The issue has progressed in Congress with a public hearing in the Constitution and Justice Committee (CCJ) of the Chamber of Deputies.

item.title: Embate pelo voto evangélico esquenta entre Lula e Flávio e nas disputas estaduais
item.description: Lula has only 21% support among evangelicals, 13 points below 2022, despite efforts such as attending cults, sending emissaries, and nominating Jorge Messias to the STF. Flávio Bolsonaro leads with 48% of this segment's support, which represents 27% of the electorate according to IBGE. The rejection is fueled by cultural conflicts, such as the Acadêmicos de Niterói parade criticizing the traditional family, and the perception that the left disrespects evangelicals.

item.title: Escolha de filho de Khamenei rompe tradição da Revolução Iraniana
item.description: The election of Mojtaba Khamenei as Iran's new supreme leader breaks the revolutionary tradition of succession based on religious merit, establishing a hereditary succession that critics like Maryam Rajavi denounce as a disguised monarchy. Despite his religious education, Mojtaba has never held public office or participated in elections, having operated only behind the scenes of power. The Assembly of Experts approved his selection by simple majority in a virtual emergency session, days after his father Ali Khamenei's death.

item.title: Moro vai ao Ceará e desafia Ciro Gomes sobre ameaça de agressão
item.description: Senator Sergio Moro (União Brasil-PR) visited Sobral (CE) and challenged Ciro Gomes (PSDB) over a 2017 threat of violence, stating he is 'not intimidated by bullies'. Moro endorsed Senator Eduardo Girão (Novo) as the gubernatorial candidate for Ceará, praising him as the only right-wing figure with the courage to confront what he described as Gomes' role in bringing the PT to power and his past remarks about receiving people 'with bullets'.

item.title: Aliados de Flávio defendem adiar apresentação de propostas após alta nas pesquisas
item.description: Allies of Flávio Bolsonaro are advocating to delay the presentation of government proposals, despite rising poll numbers, to avoid creating a 'glass ceiling' that opponents could exploit. The strategy aims to reduce public exposure to prevent increasing rejection or losing votes, focusing instead on building the ticket and defining electoral platforms. There is concern that liberal economic proposals, while well-received in financial markets, may not attract votes and could be used as ammunition by opponents, as seen with comments about revising pension and labor reforms.

item.title: Governo de SP avança na construção do Hospital de Campinas
item.description: Governor Tarcísio de Freitas formalized the donation of a 34,800 m² plot in Campinas’ Parque Itália neighborhood for the construction of the Estadual de Campinas Hospital, with an estimated investment of R$400 million. The facility will feature 262 general beds, 50 ICU beds, an eight-operating-room surgical center, and specialized services in oncology, neurosurgery, cardiology, and more, serving approximately 4.6 million residents in the region.

item.title: Dá para imaginar Flávio presidente?
item.description: Recent polls show that Senator Flávio Bolsonaro (PL/RJ), son of former President Jair Bolsonaro, has emerged as a serious presidential contender for 2026, despite initial skepticism. His candidacy was unilaterally announced by his father while the latter serves a 23-year sentence for undermining democracy, disrupting a political landscape that had previously favored Tarcísio de Freitas. Though Flávio began with a hesitant campaign focused on international right-wing support, he has gained traction in polls, narrowing the gap with front-runner Lula.

item.title: Governo já tem campanha pronta para faturar com o Oscar
item.description: The federal government has prepared a campaign to capitalize on the potential success of the Brazilian film 'O Agente Secreto' at the 2025 Oscars, with ready-to-release videos from Culture Minister Margareth Menezes for both winning and losing scenarios. The initiative aims to leverage international acclaim for Brazilian cinema to bolster the Lula administration’s image following a politically damaging week marked by polling ties with Flávio Bolsonaro and controversies involving the president’s son and the Master case. The film is nominated in four categories, including Best Picture and Best Actor for Wagner Moura.

item.title: TSE fecha cerco ao uso de inteligência artificial, mas deixa lacunas em regras para eleições de 2026
item.description: The TSE approved stricter rules for AI use in the 2026 elections, requiring social media platforms to actively remove manipulated content, but left gaps regarding accountability of AI system developers and circulation in private messaging apps. Experts warn the regulation lacks clear penalties and fails to define when AI-generated content in WhatsApp and Telegram groups constitutes electoral propaganda.

item.title: Coritiba reencontra o Remo por primeira vitória em casa no Brasileirão
item.description: Coritiba seeks its first home win in the 2025 Brasileirão against Remo, which has yet to win this season. Coritiba has seven points and strong away form, while Remo struggles with no wins in four matches. Coach Fernando Seabra will likely keep the starting XI, with Tiago Cóser replacing the suspended Jacy in defense.

item.title: Lula recebe presidente de direita boliviano após faltar à posse de presidente do mesmo campo político no Chile
item.description: President Lula hosted Bolivian President Rodrigo Paz in Brasília after canceling his attendance at the inauguration of Chilean President José Antônio Kast, sending Foreign Minister Mauro Vieira instead. The meeting focused on infrastructure, energy, border integration, and tourism, emphasizing Bolivia’s access to ports and joint efforts against organized crime in the Amazon. Despite being right-wing and attending a Trump-led summit, Paz reaffirmed Brazil as Bolivia’s main strategic partner.

item.title: Missão Planalto: por que campanha de Haddad em SP é vital para Lula
item.description: The PT has chosen Fernando Haddad as its candidate for governor of São Paulo in 2026, viewing his 2022 performance as crucial for Lula’s re-election. Although Haddad lost to Tarcísio de Freitas by 2.6 million votes in 2022, he is seen as the strongest progressive figure in the state, capable of preventing a bolsonarist sweep that could jeopardize Lula’s presidential win in a likely close and polarized race.

item.title: Cresce expectativa pela delação de Vorcaro; entenda o passo a passo
item.description: Banker Daniel Vorcaro, detained since March 4, 2026, in Operation Compliance Zero, has fueled speculation about a possible plea bargain following changes to his legal team and the leak of messages revealing his close ties with authorities. His new defense, led by lawyer José Luis Oliveira Lima (Juca), who has experience in Lava Jato plea deals, suggests he may be prepared to cooperate with the Federal Police or the Attorney General's Office, providing substantial evidence to reduce his sentence by up to two-thirds.

item.title: Entre la presidencia y las elecciones: Petro tensa las reglas y mantiene el foco de atención en plena campaña
item.description: Colombian President Gustavo Petro is maintaining an active presence on social media during the election campaign to choose his successor, despite rules prohibiting official intervention. He defends his right to respond to criticisms from presidential candidates, stating that exercising 'Politics with a capital P' is part of his daily duty. His conduct has drawn criticism from citizens, political rivals, and electoral authorities, but he refuses to step away from the campaign spotlight.

item.title: Unas elecciones municipales en Francia con aroma a presidenciales
item.description: French municipal elections are taking on presidential significance amid the decline of Macron’s government, with 36,000 municipalities voting in two rounds to test political alliances, ideologies, and the future of Macronism. The post-pandemic context and national political instability elevate the symbolic weight of these local elections, which may signal trends for the 2027 presidential race.

item.title: Joan Laporta - Víctor Font: tensión electoral en un día decisivo en el Camp Nou
item.description: The FC Barcelona presidency elections coincide with the La Liga match against Sevilla at Camp Nou on a decisive Sunday for members. The club expects high voter turnout and stadium attendance, with the inauguration of the north goal zone and Gol 1957, following a 4-1 loss in Nervión. Joan Laporta and Víctor Font are the main candidates in the race.

item.title: Com dancinha, Flávio inicia 1º giro de sua pré-campanha em RO
item.description: Senator Flávio Bolsonaro (PL-RJ) launched his first nationwide pre-campaign tour in Rondônia, where he danced with supporters during the announcement of Senator Marcos Rogério’s gubernatorial bid under the PL. Held in Ji-Paraná, the event also confirmed the PL’s Senate candidates for 2026, including federal deputy Fernando Máximo and rancher Bruno Scheid.

item.title: Flávio Bolsonaro diz que apoia CPI do Master, mas não explica ameaça
item.description: Flávio Bolsonaro stated he supports the Master Bank CPI and would sign as many as needed to investigate STF ministers, but did not explain his claim that the CPI was an attempt to 'screw over' his candidacy, despite being the 29th senator to sign the request among 36 supporters. His office denied any regret but failed to clarify how a CPI backed by political allies could be construed as a personal threat.

item.title: Haddad lança desafio sobre situação das contas públicas às vésperas de deixar governo
item.description: Finance Minister Fernando Haddad, who is about to leave Lula's government to run in this year's municipal elections, launched a public challenge on his social media to all his predecessors in the role, questioning the management of public accounts. He highlighted the improvement in fiscal indicators during his tenure and provoked former Finance Ministers to compare their performances. The move comes on the eve of his candidacy, positioning him as a defender of fiscal balance.

item.title: Lula telefona para aliados sobre terrorismo, mas ignora escândalos no Rio
item.description: Lula called the presidents of Colombia and Mexico to discuss the U.S. proposal to label Brazilian criminal factions as terrorist organizations, while overlooking local scandals in Rio de Janeiro, such as the arrest of councilman Salvino Oliveira (PSD), former youth secretary under Mayor Eduardo Paes, accused of electoral ties to drug trafficker Edgar Alves de Andrade of the Red Command. Despite Paes being a Lula ally, the president did not contact him regarding the case.

item.title: Flávio defende classificar PCC e CV como organizações terroristas
item.description: Flávio Bolsonaro advocated labeling PCC and Comando Vermelho as terrorist organizations during an event in Rondônia, claiming the federal government shields these groups by resisting their designation as terrorists by the U.S. He also criticized the PT, called for harsher penalties for violence against women, and launched his presidential pre-campaign with the goal of 'rescuing' the country from the Workers' Party.

item.title: Flávio Bolsonaro sobre Jair: aparência continua abatida, e a voz está enfraquecida
item.description: Senator Flávio Bolsonaro reported that former President Jair Bolsonaro has again experienced hiccups, a weakened voice, and a subdued appearance during a visit to DF Star Hospital in Brasília. He said Jair stated he was 'the same' and not improving, emphasizing the need for constant medical and family supervision due to medication side effects that could lead to accidents or death. Flávio is awaiting a new medical report to request house arrest from the judiciary.

item.title: Defesa apresentará novo pedido de prisão domiciliar humanitária para Bolsonaro
item.description: The defense of former President Jair Bolsonaro will submit a new request for humanitarian house arrest to Supreme Court Justice Alexandre de Moraes. The announcement was made by Senator Flávio Bolsonaro after visiting his father. The request aims to address Bolsonaro's health conditions.

item.title: Após visita, Flávio Bolsonaro diz que o pai está abatido e com sinais de cansaço
item.description: Senator Flávio Bolsonaro reported that former President Jair Bolsonaro is fatigued and showing signs of exhaustion after experiencing hiccup episodes, along with worsening kidney function and elevated inflammatory markers, according to the DFStar Hospital bulletin. Despite the clinical deterioration, Bolsonaro's condition remains stable. He was transferred from the 'Papudinha' military prison to the hospital after developing fever, nausea, and chills.

item.title: Diretora de ‘A Voz de Hind Hajab’ elogia Kleber Mendonça Filho e ‘O Agente Secreto’
item.description: Tunisian director Kaouther Ben Hania, nominated for the 2026 Oscar for 'The Voice of Hind Hajab', praised Brazilian filmmaker Kleber Mendonça Filho's 'The Secret Agent' during a panel with fellow International Feature Film nominees. She expressed deep admiration for the film’s ensemble cast, unforgettable characters, and atmospheric storytelling, saying she felt 'at home' watching it and drawing parallels to Tunisian cinema through shared themes of dictatorship and social climate.

item.title: Novo aciona Erika Hilton no Conselho de Ética por ação contra Ratinho
item.description: The Novo party filed an ethics complaint in the Chamber of Deputies against federal deputy Erika Hilton (Psol-SP) for alleged breach of decorum, claiming she systematically uses her mandate to pursue critics through legal actions over statements on gender and biological sex. The complaint follows Hilton’s successful MP-SP action against TV host Ratinho for transphobia, seeking R$10 million in moral damages to be donated to anti-transphobia efforts. Novo argues her conduct constitutes an abuse of parliamentary privileges and a violation of the Chamber’s Ethics Code.

item.title: Registro da Papudinha mostra zelo com saúde de Bolsonaro
item.description: The medical attendance record of Jair Bolsonaro at Papudinha indicates that his health is being closely monitored, contradicting statements by his son Flávio Bolsonaro, who claimed that 'they are playing with his health.' The PL presidential candidate suggested that closed-regime imprisonment would put Bolsonaro at risk of death, but the medical documents do not support this claim.

item.title: “Assinaria quantas vezes fossem necessárias”, diz Flávio sobre CPI contra ministros
item.description: Senator Flávio Bolsonaro (PL-RJ) stated he would sign the request for a parliamentary inquiry commission (CPI) against STF ministers as many times as necessary, denying a Veja magazine report claiming he regretted his signature. He reaffirmed his support for investigating the justices.

item.title: Pedro Simon diz que cadeia não ficaria mal para Moraes
item.description: Former governor and senator Pedro Simon, aged 96, stated that STF Minister Alexandre de Moraes "would not look bad in jail," describing the current moment as the "saddest page" in Brazil's history. He also criticized the Lula government, claiming its ministries lack capacity and that the country is in a permanent crisis. Simon, who supported Simone Tebet in 2022, lamented the alleged decline in respect for the Supreme Federal Court.

item.title: O plano de reação do Centrão a eventuais condenações no caso Master
item.description: Investigations into the Banco Master scandal revealed that public pension funds, tied to powerful politicians, invested millions in the scheme, involving authorities from all three branches of government. Former banker Daniel Vorcaro had direct contact with STF ministers Alexandre de Moraes and Dias Toffoli, and received support from lawmakers like Ciro Nogueira to benefit the bank. Facing pressure, Toffoli declared himself suspect, and there are efforts to bury the case, with an STF minister downplaying Vorcaro’s messages to Moraes as mere imprudence without criminal consequence.

item.title: Flávio diz que assinaria “quantas vezes fosse preciso” CPI contra ministros
item.description: Senator Flávio Bolsonaro (PL-RJ) stated he would sign as many times as necessary to support a CPI investigating ministers involved in illegality, denying a Veja report claiming he regretted backing the Toga CPI. He said his signature was intended to avoid being accused by Senator Alessandro Vieira of having 'something to hide,' noting that Vieira’s CPI request against STF ministers Alexandre de Moraes and Dias Toffoli in the Banco Master case is illegal and electorally motivated.

item.title: Motta se aproxima do governo Lula e ganha o apelido Huguevara
item.description: Hugo Motta, speaker of the Chamber of Deputados, earned the nickname 'Huguevara' after backing Lula government agendas such as ending the 6x1 work scale and regulating app-based work. His rapprochement with the president follows a period of tension, driven in part by his desire to secure Lula’s support for electing his father, Nabor Wanderley, to the Senate from Paraíba.

item.title: Ronaldo Caiado oficializa filiação ao PSD em disputa para concorrer à Presidência
item.description: Governor of Goiás, Ronaldo Caiado, officially joined the PSD in Jaraguá, competing for the party's presidential nomination alongside Ratinho Junior and Eduardo Leite. The PSD plans to announce its presidential candidate by the end of March, aiming to provide an alternative to Brazil's political polarization. According to a Genial/Quaest poll, Ratinho Jr. leads with 7% voting intention, followed by Caiado (4%) and Leite (3%).

item.title: O Lula das sortes tem poucos meios de lidar com o azar das sujeiras de Trump
item.description: The article analyzes how Donald Trump's trade war is affecting economic expectations in Brazil, particularly forecasts for a decline in the Selic interest rate. Despite initial bets on significant rate cuts by the end of 2026, the global conflict has raised rates, with the market now believing the Central Bank may not cut Selic at all (38% probability) or only make a minimal cut (55% chance for 0.25 percentage point). Uncertainty is worsened by Trump's unpredictability, which complicates Brazilian economic planning.

item.title: PL lança Marcos Rogério ao governo de Rondônia e Máximo para o Senado
item.description: Senator Marcos Rogério (PL-RO) was launched as the Liberal Party's pre-candidate for governor of Rondônia in an event in Ji-Paraná, attended by Flávio Bolsonaro. The party also confirmed Fernando Máximo and Bruno Scheid as its Senate pre-candidates. Other mentioned pre-candidates include Adailton Fúria (PSD) and Hildon Chaves (PSDB) for governor, and Confúcio Moura (MDB) and Sílvia Cristina (PP) for the Senate.
